feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: The Truth about GTA6
entry.content: Not only should every building be fully explorable, every attic, every basement, every freezer in every restaurant, every closet in every building. There is NO excuse that a game of this scale shouldn't have a standard this high, otherwise it was money laundered/corrupt, and they're ripping you off.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: The thing about Jon Bernthal is that he doesn’t look like an actor playing a tou…
entry.content: The thing about Jon Bernthal is that he doesn’t look like an actor playing a tough guy. He looks like a tough guy who accidentally became an actor. Every interview clip with him feels like somebody pulled a random guy out of a Brooklyn auto shop and discovered he’s charismatic on camera.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Console kids need to know their fanboy's are showing!
entry.content: Here's the big picture going on with the Steam Machine.... my little rant. First off, being fair, where Valve loses, is in the timing of all this, with the Hyper-inflated RAM prices coming at a time when Valve is trying to break into this console market (regardless of what the marketing material says, the whole point of tiny boxes is for the convivence of them).. An inflation that Sony dodged earlier with the PS5 and PS5 Pro. Keep in mind the PS5s have been on the market before the crash, they b

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: fun fact
entry.content: the word deli comes from the germanic word: "delicatessen" which means delicate. during ww2 german refugees would sell their food in very thin slices because they were so poor, these markets later became known as delis, because all the food there was delicate and would break very easily.

entry.title: /news/ News: It turns out Tulsi Gabbard is just a cultist, not a paid Russian shill
entry.content: https://www.washingtonpost.com/inve stigations/2026/06/21/tulsi-gabbard -her-guru-mysterious-messages-that- helped-shape-her-political-career/ https://archive.ph/vDvZN The first time I spoke with Rebecca Saltzburg, she told me Tulsi Gabbard was a freethinker who took orders from no one. “I didn’t always agree with Tulsi on everything,” Saltzburg, who worked on digital strategy for several of Gabbard’s congressional campaigns, said in November 2024. “But as for the core of her life and political

entry.title: /int/ International: anyone know anyone, anything about the Kuril Islands? Disputed volcanic island c…
entry.content: anyone know anyone, anything about the Kuril Islands? Disputed volcanic island change since WW2 with Japan claiming the southern ones and Russia having the more northern. The pictures make the nature look like some of the most amazing places on earth. Looks like you'd have to kiss some Russian ass to visit them and I'm not even sure if that would help you visit Kunashir the one I want to see that has some amazing looking coniferous forests. The japanese should work a way out to travelling to the

entry.title: /news/ News: In Sharp Rebuke to the Right Wing Uniparty, Mamdani Backed Candidates Sweep in NY elections
entry.content: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/24/ nyregion/mamdani-politics-influence .html Mayor Zohran Mamdani and his allies swept a series of congressional primaries in New York City on Tuesday in a remarkable show of strength for the insurgent left that sent shock waves through the Democratic Party. Mr. Mamdani’s candidates toppled a pair of incumbents backed by the city’s political establishment, including major labor unions and the House Democratic leader. Another candidate backed by the mayor won an o

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Have you tried stalkmaxxing and manipulationmaxxing?
entry.content: They say these days if you want to get a job in tech, networking is very important in order to get referrals. So theoretically speaking, you can park your car near a tech company everyday and pick an employee that you want to stalk by observing where they go and do in their daily life when they leave the building after their shifts. Then once you know their routine, you can fabricate your lifestyle similar to theirs and eventually meet them (like at a gym, a club, the bar, or wherever they go).

entry.title: /g/ Technology: truly linux for human beings
entry.content: - everything just works ootb - has the most guides and tutorials - has the best sw and hw compatibility - has the best font rendering - isn't outdated, nor requires updating everyday - supported and used by people with jobs, doesn't rely on community repos filled with malware - provides even more support with ubuntu pro - has the best, usable, gnome implementation - snaps are easy to remove, kubuntu minimal install comes with no snaps - makes fat stinky troon arch, gentoo, hyprland users seethe

entry.title: /g/ Technology: hey fa/g/s anyone of you has worked on a virtual museum before? or should I ask…
entry.content: hey fa/g/s anyone of you has worked on a virtual museum before? or should I ask the /3/tards about my project? Ive asking zoomers and boomers IRL and they seem to like the idea of a virtual museum app but I feel like they are lying because most of these cunts spend more time on facebook or tiktok than actually looking for museums, let alone a virtual museum app. Is this actually a viable project, or am I just building a shit nobody is going to use?

entry.title: /o/ Auto: I don't like trucks
entry.content: Not because you can't see a row of children sitting in front of them (idk what does it even have to do with any real world scenario). Not because they're absolute gas guzzlers, even though global warming is pretty disturbing. I don't like them because I feel bad for a V8 engine that could be put in a muscle car and rip through Autobahns as free as a bird but instead it ended up in a pigfat aerodynamic nightmare that can't go fast and 95% of the time it doesn't even do truck stuff.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Microsoft is legit retarded. Set aside the fact that there are millions of 10 ye…
entry.content: Microsoft is legit retarded. Set aside the fact that there are millions of 10 year olds yelling at them to fix Bedrock on Twitter, set aside the fact that there are 2 different implementations that work completely differently for no good reason, set aside the 3 garbage spinoff games, set aside the movies with Jack Black and just tell me if there is any salvaging this game. I want to go a time when I thought the update with the dragon was dumb and tacky.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Why is it whenever a game has fantasy races theres always this huge societal, ge…
entry.content: Why is it whenever a game has fantasy races theres always this huge societal, generational and cultural divide between them all? You’ll get elves who hate mankind or think they’re above mankind, men who have no regard for nature or elves, dwarves who hate elves, beast races who hate humans and then so on with others like orcs, ogres, goblins and trolls.

Stand by me and support the movement. The court still thinks i'm an anti, and I'm always taking shit from them so they can cover for you. You are shooting yourselves in the foot. Nobody else is putting themselves on the line. Please, believe that I'm really on your side. ^__^' <<33

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: What time zone is it in Antarctica? The sun rises and sets here only once a year…
entry.content: What time zone is it in Antarctica? The sun rises and sets here only once a year. It's permanent daytime. The continent is full of research bases, but only about 5,000 people. No one lives here permanently, which is why the time here is whatever people decide. Turns out they usually choose the time zone of whoever gives them food, their supply base. Basically, down here, there's no official time zone in Antarctica. This is the only continent on Earth without one.

entry.title: /g/ Technology: This is an oddly specific request, but maybe I can get some assistance here. Doe…
entry.content: This is an oddly specific request, but maybe I can get some assistance here. Does anyone with Ting Mobile have access to RCS on their iPhones? I can use it on my Android phones (both old and new), but the iPhone (17) says "Waiting for activation..." in the RCS Messaging Settings. There are relatively recent reddit posts discussing it, but nothing newer than ~4months ago and I'm not clear if it's an issue with iPhones specifically or just me. https://www.reddit.com/r/ting/comme nts/1rohmyr/frustr

entry.title: /g/ Technology: C++ virtuals + inheritance as a metaphor is boomer-coded and that's why it's fragile
entry.content: I mean just think about it. You have a base class - the boomer - and instead of passing everything down as it would with normal inheritance (where each section starts from humble beginnings and increases in wealth and capability with each subsequent generation) you get this degenerate form of inheritance where the boomer owns almost all of the data and functionality, gobbles up even more, then the parents own less so, then the child owns almost nothing and ends at a terminal point where there ar

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >"I remember when [the first Black Sabbath] album came out," Iommi recalled, "An…
entry.content: >"I remember when [the first Black Sabbath] album came out," Iommi recalled, "And every single review of it without exception was negative. We were slammed so hard that for a moment I was completely baffled and couldn't understand what we did wrong. As the weeks went by and the album started climbing up the charts, I felt a huge sigh of relief. After that we never read a review or paid attention to what critics said ever again."

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Reddit botnet
entry.content: I want to control narratives on reddit and other social media platforms. Reddit as it is full of literal apes that decide their opinions based on what has more internet points and how many people agree or disagree with them. It seems the easiest to influence. What are good resources and forums for teaching myself how to build a botnet/mass control accounts to comment/like/dislike content?
